name: Derry
content:
Derry is located in Northern Ireland. The British know it as Londonderry. The area is named after the hit TV show Derry Girls. Like many people who moved to Ireland, I did not understand Derry Girls until I had lived there for around a year, after which time the show made perfect sense.
Allegedly, the architecture in Derry is very interesting, or so I was told by a German study abroad student I met by the river near Offing Coffee. She was studying in County Mayo at the time, but would visit Derry often to look at the architecture more. Personally I think the architecture in Derry is alright, though perhaps I don’t have the training to properly appreciate the architecture of Derry.
I was walking around downtown Derry one morning, dressed in my travel clothes as usual, killing time until my bus to Dublin was set to depart, when I was stopped by a man several years my senior. It is important to note that, while I had become accustomed to the Limerick and milder Dublin Irish accents at this point, I still had trouble understanding the Northern Irish accents found in Derry. On his third attempt, I was finally able to understand that he was asking me if I was looking for a shift. I quickly explained that I didn’t live in Derry and wasn’t looking for a job, to which he replied “so do you want to make out?” Ah, so that’s what “looking for a shift” means! To his credit, the Derry man was very polite about the whole interaction and my declining his proposition, but the interaction did leave me confused as to why it happened at 10 AM on a Saturday morning on a main street along downtown while I was wearing jeans, a t-shirt, and my backpack loaded with my travel supplies. Sometimes I wonder if I could have gotten him to take me out to breakfast.
